meaning: n.澶氭煴寮忓缓绛慳dj.澶氭煴寮忓缓绛戠殑
examples: The cult temple typically included a massive pylon entrance with a court leading to a hypostyle hall and at the heart of the temple a shrine for the cult image.
The cult temple typically included a massive pylon entrance with a court leading to a hypostyle hall and, at the heart of the temple, a shrine for the cult image.
You can fill entire days seeing Karnak, the largest religious site in the world. Inside, in the Great Hypostyle Hall, you can find 134 stone columns covered in hieroglyphics.
